doc_title: How to Run a Product Discovery Interview Without Leading the Witness

scenes.title: Intro: What Is Leading the Witness
scenes.visual_metaphor: A warm flat-vector studio with a large framed question mark center-stage. Two stylized speech bubbles -- one labeled 'You' (left, cool blue) and one labeled 'User' (right, warm orange) -- face each other across a desk. When a leading question is asked, red sticky-note labels fly out of the You-bubble and slap onto the User-bubble before it can respond.

beats.phrase: Hey guys, hello everyone.
beats.visual_action: Camera fades in on the studio set; the question-mark frame slides up from below; both speech bubbles drift in from opposite sides.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Establish the visual environment and the two-party conversation dynamic that will be revisited throughout the video.

beats.phrase: Welcome back to my channel.
beats.visual_action: A subscribe-bell glyph pings next to the frame, then settles.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Quick channel-brand cue so the viewer orients to a familiar explainer format before the content begins.

beats.phrase: Today I wanted to address a very basic question.
beats.visual_action: The question mark inside the frame glows softly and scales up slightly.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Visually flags that a question is being posed, priming the viewer for the problem statement.

beats.phrase: How do you run a product discovery interview without basically planting answers in the
beats.visual_action: The You-bubble emits three red seed icons that arc across and embed into the User-bubble's surface.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Concretizes 'planting answers' as objects physically transferred from interviewer to user, making the abstract metaphor tangible.

beats.phrase: user's head?
beats.visual_action: The seeds sink into the User-bubble; a subtle ripple radiates from each impact point.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Completes the planting metaphor by showing the answer taking root inside the user's headspace.

beats.phrase: So what does that mean leading the witness?
beats.visual_action: The red seeds flash; a label tag reading 'Leading the Witness' stamps onto the frame below the question mark.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Names the concept just introduced so the viewer has a handle for the pattern about to be exemplified.

beats.phrase: It's when you ask a question like, don't you think this checkout flow is
beats.visual_action: The You-bubble opens; text forms 'Don't you think this checkout flow is' while two red opinion-tags -- 'confusing' implied -- hover above the gap.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Shows the interviewer's words pre-loaded with an opinion, setting up the reveal that the answer was handed over.

beats.phrase: confusing?
beats.visual_action: The red tag 'confusing' drops into the User-bubble before it has any chance to form its own text.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Demonstrates the exact moment a leading word lands in the user's mouth before they can think for themselves.

beats.phrase: Right there, you've told them what to feel.
beats.visual_action: A red highlight sweeps across the settled tag; the User-bubble's own text area dims and goes inert.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Makes clear the consequence: the user's independent thought is suppressed because the feeling was pre-supplied.

beats.phrase: Let's take an example to explain this.
beats.visual_action: The current example bubbles slide off left; a fresh pair of bubbles slides in, resetting for a new case study.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Provides a clean visual transition signaling a new concrete example is coming, so the viewer does not conflate the two.

scenes.hook: When you ask a leading question, you plant the answer before the user can think.

scenes.title: Bad vs. Good Question Structure
scenes.visual_metaphor: Same two-bubble conversation layout, but now a label bar above the bubbles toggles between a red 'CLOSED / LEADING' state and a green 'OPEN / NEUTRAL' state. When red, the You-bubble fires a pre-filled answer pill into the User-bubble. When green, the You-bubble sends an open doorway icon, and genuine thought-bubbles organically grow out of the User-bubble.

beats.phrase: If I say, was the onboarding hard?
beats.visual_action: Label bar flips to red 'CLOSED / LEADING'; the You-bubble sends a pill tagged 'hard' directly into the User-bubble.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Shows a closed yes/no question as a packaged answer the user merely accepts rather than generates.

beats.phrase: The user will almost always say yes, because you handed them the answer.
beats.visual_action: The User-bubble instantly echoes 'yes' with a green check; the 'hard' pill dissolves into its word-cloud, coloring it red.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Reveals the causal chain: the supplied word becomes the user's response, contaminating the data.

beats.phrase: Instead, ask, walk me through your first time using the product.
beats.visual_action: Label bar flips to green 'OPEN / NEUTRAL'; the You-bubble sends an open doorway icon; no answer pills are present.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Contrasts structurally: an open question is depicted as an entry point the user walks through, not a pre-filled container.

beats.phrase: That's open-ended, neutral, and you learn what actually matters to them.
beats.visual_action: From the User-bubble, three organic thought-clouds grow at different sizes labeled with genuine topics; they glow warmly.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Shows that neutrality lets the user's real priorities surface organically, which is the goal of discovery.

beats.phrase: Now, here's something I've developed over many, many interviews.
beats.visual_action: The thought-clouds and bubbles recede; a new centered panel slides in with three empty slots arranged horizontally.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Signals a transition from principles to a concrete repeatable framework the viewer can learn.

beats.phrase: I call it echo, pause, probe.
beats.visual_action: Three icons lock into the slots left-to-right: a curved echo arrow, a pause-bar pair, and a magnifier; the label 'Echo · Pause · Probe' stamps beneath.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Establishes the three-step mnemonic visually so the viewer has a mental scaffold for the detailed breakdown that follows.

fill="#1c8b7c">Echo · Pause · Probe</text></g></svg>
scenes.hook: Closed questions hand users the answer. Open ones earn the truth.

scenes.title: The Echo Pause Probe Technique
scenes.visual_metaphor: The three-icon framework bar (echo arrow, pause bars, magnifier) stays pinned at the top. Below it, the two-bubble conversation returns. As each step activates, its icon lights up and a corresponding visual mechanism fires between the bubbles: words detach and echo back (echo), a countdown timer ring fills with silence waves (pause), a magnifier descends onto a thought-cloud (probe).

beats.phrase: First, echo.
beats.visual_action: The echo-arrow icon lights up amber; the other two remain dim.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Directs the viewer's attention to step one of the framework.

beats.phrase: Repeat their last phrase back.
beats.visual_action: The last three words inside the User-bubble detach, slide left, and dock into the You-bubble.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Concretizes 'echo' as a physical reflection of words back to the speaker.

beats.phrase: If they say, it felt slow, you say, it felt slow.
beats.visual_action: The phrase 'it felt slow' clones from the User-bubble into the You-bubble; both copies glow briefly to show they are identical.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Demonstrates the verbatim mirroring technique so the viewer sees exactly what an echo sounds like in practice.

beats.phrase: Then, pause.
beats.visual_action: The pause-bars icon lights up amber; the echo arrow dims.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Shifts focus to step two of the framework.

beats.phrase: This is the hard part.
beats.visual_action: A small difficulty badge -- a wrench -- stamps next to the pause icon.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Flags that this step is counter-intuitive and requires discipline, pre-empting the viewer's discomfort with silence.

beats.phrase: Sit in silence for three full seconds.
beats.visual_action: A circular countdown timer ring appears between the bubbles and fills over three seconds; concentric silence-wave rings ripple outward.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Makes the duration of productive silence visible and countable, so the viewer internalizes how long three seconds actually feels.

beats.phrase: Don't rush to fill it.
beats.visual_action: A faint ghost word tries to form in the You-bubble but is blocked by a red X; it dissolves.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Visually warns against the instinct to break silence, reinforcing restraint.

beats.phrase: People hate awkward silence, so they'll keep talking and go deeper.
beats.visual_action: The User-bubble generates a new, larger thought-cloud that pushes deeper downward, revealing a second layer of content beneath the surface layer.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Shows the payoff of silence: the user volunteers richer, more honest information than the surface answer.

beats.phrase: Then, probe.
beats.visual_action: The magnifier icon lights up amber; the pause bars dim.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Shifts focus to step three of the framework.

beats.phrase: Ask something neutral like, can you tell me more about that?
beats.visual_action: A magnifier descends from the You-bubble onto the deeper thought-cloud; the cloud expands and brightens.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Depicts a neutral probe as a tool that illuminates existing user content rather than injecting new content.

beats.phrase: But having said that, don't overuse it every 30 seconds, or it starts feeling
beats.visual_action: A metronome ticks rapidly; a warning overlay cracks across the framework bar; the icons flicker in a stiff repeating pattern.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Signals overuse as a mechanical, robotic failure mode so the viewer knows the technique has limits.

beats.phrase: robotic.
beats.visual_action: The three icons lock into a rigid robot-eye grid; a cold blue tint washes over them.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Punctuates the consequence: mechanical repetition kills rapport.

beats.phrase: Use it when they say something genuinely interesting.
beats.visual_action: The robot grid shatters; a single thought-cloud in the User-bubble pulses with a warm starburst glow; the framework icons relax back to neutral.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Restores the technique's proper context: deploy it selectively when the user reveals something worth exploring.

scenes.hook: Three steps to make people reveal what they actually think.

scenes.title: Be a Mirror Not a Magnifying Glass
scenes.visual_metaphor: The You-bubble transforms into a large rectangular object. A split-screen test runs down the middle: the left half shows the object as a flat mirror reflecting the User-bubble's content back unchanged (mirror mode). The right half shows it as a magnifying glass zooming into one pre-selected detail of the User-bubble (magnifying-glass mode). The mirror side glows green; the magnifier side glows red.

beats.phrase: The golden rule is this.
beats.visual_action: A golden border stamp drops around the entire frame; the split-screen mirror/magnifier object rises into center view.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Signals a key principle is coming and establishes the dual visual analogy that will carry the rule.

beats.phrase: Your job in a discovery interview is to be a mirror, not a magnifying
beats.visual_action: Left half lights green: the mirror surface reflects the User-bubble's words exactly, undistorted. Right half lights red: a lens zooms into one fragment.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Contrasts the two roles side by side so the viewer can see the difference between reflecting and amplifying.

beats.phrase: class.
beats.visual_action: The word 'glass' completes on the right side; the lens clicks into place with a red lock.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Completes the phrase and locks the negative analogy visually.

beats.phrase: Reflect what they say.
beats.visual_action: The green mirror side brightens; the user's words appear on its surface, identical and unaltered.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Reinforces the positive behavior: faithful, undistorted reflection of the user's own content.

beats.phrase: Don't zoom in to what you want to hear.
beats.visual_action: The red magnifier side enlarges a single pre-tagged fragment while the rest of the user's content shrinks and grays out.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Shows the distortion caused by confirmation bias: zooming in amplifies only what the interviewer hoped to find.

beats.phrase: Needless to say, this is harder than it sounds, because as product managers, we
beats.visual_action: A silhouette of a PM enters from the left carrying a stack of pre-written hypothesis cards; the cards emit a faint pull-force toward the magnifier side.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Introduces the source of the bias -- pre-existing hypotheses -- as physical objects that actively tug toward the magnifying-glass behavior.

beats.phrase: already have hypotheses about what the problem is.
beats.visual_action: The hypothesis cards fan out; each card's label (e.g., 'too slow', 'missing feature') glows and drifts toward the magnifier lens.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Makes the hypotheses tangible and shows them as the gravitational force pulling the interviewer away from neutral mirroring.

scenes.hook: Reflect what they say. Don't zoom in to what you want to hear.

scenes.title: The Payoff and Outro
scenes.visual_metaphor: The mirror/magnifier split object remains but now the PM silhouette actively pushes the hypothesis cards away. As the cards dissolve, the mirror surface expands to full width and the user's content grows richer and more detailed, glowing with warm authenticity. The scene ends in the studio from Scene 1 with the question-mark frame and a sign-off card.

beats.phrase: But if you can suppress that instinct, the insights you get will be 10
beats.visual_action: The PM silhouette pushes the hypothesis cards away; they shatter into dust; the mirror surface expands to full width.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Shows the deliberate effort required to override bias and the reward: the mirror expands, enabling full-fidelity reflection.

beats.phrase: times more authentic.
beats.visual_action: The user's thought-clouds on the mirror surface multiply and glow ten times brighter; a '10×' badge stamps onto the frame.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Quantifies the payoff visually so the viewer feels the magnitude of the reward for discipline.

beats.phrase: I hope you find this video helpful.
beats.visual_action: The mirror object and bubbles recede; the studio backdrop from Scene 1 returns with the question-mark frame.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Provides visual closure by returning to the opening environment, signaling the content portion is complete.

beats.phrase: Let me know in the comments how you find this content.
beats.visual_action: A comment-bubble icon pops below the frame with a cursor arrow hovering over it.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Standard but clear call-to-action visual that directs viewer behavior.

beats.phrase: If there are any things you want me to touch upon, that would also
beats.visual_action: A lightbulb icon appears next to the comment bubble; a small pencil taps it, suggesting future topics.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Invites viewer participation by visualizing the suggestion mechanism.

beats.phrase: be great to know.
beats.visual_action: The lightbulb brightens and a small heart pings out of it.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Warm visual cue that reinforces appreciation for viewer input.

beats.phrase: Thank you, and I will see you in the next video.
beats.visual_action: A sign-off card slides up: 'Thanks for watching' with a forward-arrow icon pointing right.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Clean visual farewell that matches the verbal sign-off.

beats.phrase: Bye!
beats.visual_action: The scene fades to a soft solid color as the arrow exits right.
beats.pedagogical_purpose: Provides a clean end-frame so the video concludes without visual clutter.

scenes.hook: Stop pitching solutions. Start probing reality. 10x more authentic insights.
